message:
fuzz: add pdu_stream_decode target for codec::Pdu::stream_decode

Adds a libfuzzer harness that drives the WezTerm-derived mux-protocol
decoder (`codec::Pdu::stream_decode`) with attacker-controlled bytes,
closing a fuzz-coverage gap on one of the most untrusted input
surfaces in the mux stack: framed PDUs arriving from a peer mux
server.

## Scope of the target

`fuzz/fuzz_targets/pdu_stream_decode.rs` feeds up to 64 KiB of
libfuzzer input into a growing buffer, then calls `stream_decode`
up to 8 times per iteration. Each iteration either:

- decodes a full PDU (we touch `serial` and `pdu_name()` to force
  the lazy fields and surface downstream UB),
- returns `Ok(None)` indicating "need more bytes" (we break — this
  is the success path for short inputs and is not interesting to
  hammer further), or
- returns an error (we break — malformed input is fine; what we
  care about is that the decoder handles it without panicking or
  reading uninitialized memory).

## Why the iteration cap

The `buffer.len() >= before_len` guard at the bottom of the loop
catches decoder implementations that would otherwise not consume any
bytes and cause an infinite loop under libfuzzer. If no forward
progress is made on an iteration, we break out deterministically.
The 8-iteration cap further bounds per-input cost so the fuzzer
keeps a high corpus-throughput rate.

## Wiring

- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` gains the new `[[bin]]` stanza
  `name = "pdu_stream_decode"` pointing at the new file, plus
  a `codec = { path = "../frankenterm/codec" }` dependency.
  `codec` lives under the vendored WezTerm tree and was not
  previously a fuzz-crate dep — this adds it.
- `Cargo.lock` picks up one corresponding entry: `codec` as a
  dependency of `frankenterm-fuzz`. Already-resolved; no version
  churn anywhere else.

## Running

    cd fuzz
    cargo +nightly fuzz run pdu_stream_decode -- -max_len=65536

The `-max_len` cap matches the target's `MAX_INPUT_LEN` so
libfuzzer does not produce inputs the harness would immediately
reject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
